location.office_time_required: 4 days a week in one of our SF Bay Area offices

level_signals: 8-10+ years of experience in technical program management
Base salary range $198,000 - $235,500 consistent with Senior IC at a late-stage startup
Leads complex, cross-functional initiatives spanning multi-quarter programs

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: BS/MS in Computer Science, Engineering, or a related technical field.
8-10+ years of experience in technical program management with a proven track record of driving complex product programs from concept to launch in fast-paced SaaS environments.
Deep technical background in AI/ML systems, LLMs, and agents, with an ability to translate complex capabilities into practical product impact.
Strong understanding of software product development, including API design, data systems, and product integration patterns.
Ability to structure multi-quarter product programs with measurable outcomes.
Excellent written and verbal communication skills; able to translate between technical and business contexts.
Demonstrated ability to manage through ambiguity, anticipate risks, and build alignment across diverse teams.
A builder mindset; proactive, collaborative, and focused on enabling teams to deliver their best work.

scope.surface: Enterprise AI product roadmap execution — search, AI assistant, and agentic capabilities on Glean's Work AI platform
primary_metrics_inferred: Product launch quality and adoption impact
Customer adoption of new product capabilities
core_responsibilities: Lead end-to-end product development programs, spanning ideation, design, engineering, and launch.
Build and maintain program plans that drive clarity across teams ensuring transparency in scope, timelines, and ownership.
Drive execution excellence for cross-functional initiatives, balancing speed with quality.
Partner with Go-To-Market and Customer Success teams to ensure readiness for product rollouts and customer adoption.
Facilitate communication across Product, Design, and Engineering to align priorities and unblock delivery.
Proactively identify and mitigate delivery risks, tradeoffs, and cross-team dependencies.
Establish clear visibility into execution health using metrics such as velocity, quality, and adoption impact.
Continuously evolve how Glean plans, tracks, and delivers cross-functional programs thereby improving predictability and accountability.
Driving the company's Product roadmap execution, ensuring alignment between product vision, engineering delivery, and customer outcomes.
Structuring and managing programs for major product launches, platform enhancements, and customer-facing capabilities.
Partnering with PMs and Eng to define clear requirements, success metrics, and launch criteria.
Anticipating risks, resolving dependencies, and ensuring smooth execution across engineering and GTM teams.
Improving execution rigor, building visibility into progress, blockers, and outcomes across multiple teams.
Driving operational excellence through consistent program cadences, postmortems, and lessons learned.

compensation.equity_form: Certain roles may be eligible for variable compensation, equity, and benefits

green_flags: Compensation range disclosed and competitive ($198K-$235.5K base)
Clear product surface and ownership area (enterprise AI platform, roadmap execution)
Fast-growing company with strong market recognition (Fast Company Top 10, Forbes AI 50, etc.)
Role sits at high-visibility intersection of product, engineering, design, and GTM
Comprehensive benefits package including home office stipend, education and wellness stipends, 401k
red_flags: Hybrid requirement of 4 days/week in SF Bay Area office is very high and eliminates most non-local candidates
No mention of visa sponsorship — unclear for international candidates
Buzzword-heavy JD with limited specificity on team structure or direct reporting line
BS/MS in CS/Engineering listed as hard requirement with no mention of equivalent experience accepted

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from comp range and years of experience)
scope.business_model (inferred from product description and customer base)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not explicitly stated)
ambiguities: The JD states 'Certain roles may be eligible for variable compensation, equity' — it is unclear whether this TPM role specifically includes equity or variable comp.
Education requirement lists BS/MS but does not explicitly state whether equivalent experience is accepted; treated as hard with no equivalent experience accepted given the unconditional phrasing.
